P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
(916) 322·5662 
Administration 
322·5660 
Executive/legal 
32~·5901 
Enforcement 
322-6441 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Robert B. Pye 
Hackard I Hayes, 
1435 River Park 
Sacramento, CA 
Dear Mr. Pye: 
October 25, 1983 
Pye and Longyear 
Drive, Suite 405 
95815 
Re: 
Your Request for Written 
Advice; Our No. A-83-224 
Your letter requests advice on behalf of your client 
Sacramento County Supervisor Ted Sheedy. 
Your letter recites 
the following facts: 
FACTS 
1. 
Sacramento County Supervisor Ted Sheedy (Sheedy) is 
an employee, pursuant to Agreement dated September 7, 
1983, of Caravel Travel (Caravel) which is situated in 
and doing business in Sacrmento, County, California. 
2. 
Caravel is a California, for-profit company which 
arranges transportation, lodging and other services 
associated with business and recreational travel. 
Caravel receives a commission on the amount of air 
fare, lodging and other services. 
3. 
Sheedy is an employee, not an officer, of Caravel. 
He does not own stock or have any other proprietary 
interest in Caravel. 
Sheedy will be paid a straight 
salary of $2,200.00, which will compensate him for his 
services to Caravel in analyzing tour packages, serving 
as an English speaking guide in China, and promoting 
tours by speaking to groups organized by Caravel from 
September 1983 until March 1984. 
4. 
Sheedy arranged a China tour for Caravel in which 
he was responsible for signing up tour participants. 
As compensation for this service, Caravel agreed to pay 
Sheedy a commission of One Hundred Dollars ($100.00) 
per part ic ipant. 


Robe rt B. Pye 
October 25, 1983 
Page 2 
5. 
It is likely that either Caravel and/or one or more 
tour participants will come before the Board of 
Supervisors within the next 12 months with an 
application which is subject to the Board's 
determination. 
ANALYSIS 
I enclose and incorporate by reference the advice letter to 
Mr. L. B. Elam, Sacramento County Counsel, relating to somewhat 
similar circumstances involving another Sacramento County 
Supervisor, Sandra Smoley. 
(Advice letter No. A-82-054.) 
The 
analysis therein as to "Question No.1" is applicable to 
Mr. Sheedy's situation as well, and should be reviewed by you. 
Caravel is a source of income to Mr. Sheedy of $250 or more 
and will remain so for a period extending 12 months beyond the 
last payment or payments equalling $250 or more. 
Government 
Code Section 87103(c) .1/ 
In addition, during the period of 
his employment Mr. Sheedy will be an employee of Caravel. 
Section 87103(d). 
Consequently, Mr. Sheedy will have to 
disqualify himself as to any official decision which will have a 
reasonably foreseeable material financial effect upon Caravel. 
Sections 87100 and 87103. 
These terms are further refined in 
the Commission's regulations 2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18700, 
18702, copies of which are enclosed. 
The $100 commission received by Mr. Sheedy makes the 
participants in the China trip sources of income to him as 
well.l! 
However, before a source of income creates a 
potential for disqualification, it must equal or exceed $250. 
Consequently, individual participants will not create the 
potential for disqualification. 
However, if a company or other 
purchases several places on such a trip, thereby becoming a 
source of $250 or more in commission income, then it would be a 
source of income requiring disqualification under Section 
87103(c). 
Mr. Sheedy would again have a 12-month period in 
which disqualification would be required as to any decision 
which would have a reasonably foreseeable material financial 
effect upon any such source of income. 
1/ All statutory references are to the Government Code 
unless otherwise specified. 
1/ See the analysis on "Question No.1" in the ~ 
advice letter for a full explanation of this point. 


Robert B. Pye 
October 25, 1983 
Page 3 
I trust this letter is responsive to your client's needs. 
As further facts develop, you may wish to contact us for further 
advice. 
Should you have any questions regarding this advice, 
please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
REL:plh 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
Robert E. Leidigh 
Counsel 
Legal Division 


HACKARD, HAYES, PYE & LONGYEAR 
A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION 
MICHAEL A. HACKARD 
]. CHAUNCEY HAYES 
ROBERT B. PYE 
VAN LONGYEAR 
Ms. Barbara Milan 
State of California 
1435 RIVER PARK DRIVE, SUITE 405 
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95815 
(916) 929-5545 
September 27, 1983 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Post Office Box 807 
Sacramento, Ca. 95804 
Dear Ms. Milan: 
We request on behalf of Sacramento County Supervisor Ted Sheedy, 
your written advice as to any conflict of interest which he faces 
and any resulting duty to disqualify himself from the decision 
making process or the County Board of Supervisors. 
This request 
is made based on the facts as set out below. 
1. 
Sacramento County Supervisor Ted Sheedy (Sheedy) is an em-
ployee, pursuant to Agreement dated Setpember 7, 1983, of Caravel 
Travel (Caravel) which is situated in and doing business in 
Sacramento County, California. 
2. 
Caravel is a Califronia, for-profit company which arranges 
transportation, lodging and other services associated with bus-
iness and recreational travel. 
Caravel receives a commission 
on the amount of air fare, lodging and other services. 
3. 
Sheedy is an employee, not an officer, of Caravel. 
He does 
not own stock or have any other proprietary interest in Caravel. 
Sheedy will be paid a straight salary of $2,200.00, which will 
compensate him for his services to Caravel in analyzing tour 
packages, serving as an English speaking guide in China, and 
promoting tours by speaking to groups organized by Caraval 
from September 1983 until March 1984. 
4. 
Sheedy arranged a China tour for Caravel in which he was 
responsible for signing up tour participants. 
As compensation 
for this service, Caravel agreed to pay Sheedy a commission of 
One Hundred Doll~rs ($100.00) per participant. 
5. 
It is likely that either Caravel and/or one or more tour 
participants will come before the Board of Supervisors within 
the next 12 months with an application which is subject to the 
Board's determination. 


Ms. Barbara Milan 
September 27, 1983 
Page 2 
Mr. Sheedy would like to be assured that he may reasonably 
rely upon your opinion, so should any questions arise or 
should you need any further information, please do not 
hesitate to contact me. 
Very truly yours, 
HACKARD, HAYES, PYE & LONGYEAR, 
A Professional Law Corporation 
ROBERT B. PYE 
RBP/km 


tate of Califorrzia 
Fair Political Practices CommissioIl.

Robert B. Pye

322·5660 
October 12, 1983 
1435 River Park Drive, Suite 405 
Sacramento 
CA 
95815 
Re: 
A-83-224 
r Mr. P'je: 
322·6441 
Your 
tter requesting advice under the Political 
form Act has been referred to Robert E. Leidigh, an 
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at 
(916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex 
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to 
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21 
working days.

sections.question: Whether Sacramento County Supervisor Ted Sheedy faces any conflict of interest based on his employment with Caravel Travel, including a salary of $2,200 and a commission of $100 per tour participant for a China tour, given that Caravel and/or tour participants may come before the Board of Supervisors within 12 months with applications subject to the Board's determination.
sections.conclusion: Caravel is a source of income to Mr. Sheedy of $250 or more and will remain so for a period extending 12 months beyond the last payment or payments equalling $250 or more. Consequently, Mr. Sheedy will have to disqualify himself as to any official decision which will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect upon Caravel. Individual tour participants will not create the potential for disqualification unless a company or entity purchases several places on a trip, thereby becoming a source of $250 or more in commission income, in which case disqualification would be required for decisions having a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect upon such sources of income.

sections.question_synthetic: Does Sacramento County Supervisor Ted Sheedy have a conflict of interest requiring disqualification from decisions regarding Caravel Travel or its customers, based on his employment agreement and commission compensation arrangement?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Supervisor Sheedy must disqualify himself from decisions with a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on Caravel Travel under Government Code sections 87100 and 87103. Individual tour participants earning less than $250 in commissions do not trigger disqualification, but entities purchasing multiple tour places that exceed the $250 threshold would require disqualification for 12 months.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (dated September 27, 1983) and the FPPC advice response letter (dated October 25, 1983). The question and conclusion sections are derived from the FPPC response. A preliminary acknowledgment letter from October 12, 1983 is also included. Confidence is high due to clear structure and explicit conclusion section.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Sacramento County Supervisor Ted Sheedy faces any conflict of interest based on his employment with Caravel Travel, including a salary of $2,200 and a commission of $100 per tour participant for a China tour, given that Caravel and/or tour participants may come before the Board of Supervisors within 12 months with applications subject to the Board's determination.

CONCLUSION: Caravel is a source of income to Mr. Sheedy of $250 or more and will remain so for a period extending 12 months beyond the last payment or payments equalling $250 or more. Consequently, Mr. Sheedy will have to disqualify himself as to any official decision which will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect upon Caravel. Individual tour participants will not create the potential for disqualification unless a company or entity purchases several places on a trip, thereby becoming a source of $250 or more in commission income, in which case disqualification would be required for decisions having a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect upon such sources of income.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that Sacramento County Supervisor Ted Sheedy must disqualify himself from Board decisions affecting his employer Caravel Travel, which is a $250+ income source, and similarly for any tour participant group that generates $250 or more in commissions to him.
